summary: Demis Hassabis discusses the natural constraints slowing down AI progress, such as hardware shortages and increased research secrecy due to commercialization. He suggests this deceleration might be beneficial, providing society more time to address the philosophical and commercial implications of AGI. He also advocates for using AI to drive scientific breakthroughs in areas like climate change to demonstrate its real-world value.

principles.statement: A slower pace of AI development might be beneficial for society.
principles.rationale: We need more time to sort out the complex commercial and philosophical issues surrounding AI before we reach Artificial General Intelligence (AGI).
principles.source_quote: Look, it may be a good thing that it’s not as fast. There’s a whole bunch of other things that we need to think through with this technology... We don’t have a lot of time to sort out before we get to [Artificial General Intelligence].

principles.statement: The AI industry must focus more on generating scientific breakthroughs to solve global challenges.
principles.rationale: Developing new technologies is one of the only viable ways to tackle massive issues like climate change in a fragmented political world, and it helps demonstrate AI's positive real-world impact.
principles.source_quote: One of the only ways to tackle climate in today’s fragmented political world is to come up with some new technologies. I actually think AI as an industry needs to be doing more of that.

principles.statement: AI's trajectory requires broad societal debate.
principles.rationale: Because AI is a general-purpose technology that will affect every aspect of life, all parts of society must be involved in discussing its implementation and guardrails.
principles.source_quote: AI is going to affect everything, so I think it does need to be debated, by all parts of society...

mental_models.name: Balancing Serving with Training
mental_models.description: A lens for understanding resource allocation in the commercial AI era. Compute resources and focus can no longer be dedicated solely to research and training new models; they must be balanced against the massive infrastructure required to serve existing models to millions of users.
mental_models.example: Google DeepMind shifting from a pure research lab to transforming Google's core products, requiring them to balance the invention of new tech with running AI for ever-growing numbers of users.

signature_quotes.text: Look, it may be a good thing that it’s not as fast. There’s a whole bunch of other things that we need to think through with this technology.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing the natural constraints slowing down the acceleration of AI development.

signature_quotes.text: We’re now also in the era of full commercialization of these systems, so you’ve also got to balance serving with training.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining how the AI boom has reshaped the dynamics of research and resource allocation.

signature_quotes.text: Right now, it’s memory chips, but it’ll probably be something else tomorrow.
signature_quotes.context: Commenting on the physical and supply-chain bottlenecks constraining AI scaling.

signature_quotes.text: There’s so much commercial pressure, so some of those things can’t be shared quite as openly anymore, which is a shame on the one hand, but it’s understandable.
signature_quotes.context: Reflecting on the decline of open research sharing in the AI industry.

signature_quotes.text: One of the only ways to tackle climate in today’s fragmented political world is to come up with some new technologies. I actually think AI as an industry needs to be doing more of that.
signature_quotes.context: Advocating for the use of AI in scientific discovery to solve real-world problems.

signature_quotes.text: There were only a few dozen researchers working on AI properly and we all knew each other. We were, in most cases, really good friends.
signature_quotes.context: Reminiscing about the early, pre-commercialization days of AI research.

anti_patterns.description: Assuming that research secrecy does not negatively affect the pace of AI progress.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Commercial pressures force companies to keep research private, which slows down the cross-pollination of ideas that historically helped AI evolve from a research backwater into a hotbed of breakthroughs.
